title: BurstDECONV GUI
author: Rachel Topno
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
BurstDECONV is a novel statistical inference method that deconvolves signal traces into individual  transcription initiation events.  We use the distribution of waiting times between successive polymerase initiation  events to identify mechanistic features of transcription   
such as the number of rate-limiting steps and their kinetics. This repository contains the BurstDECONV graphical user interface implemented for Windows and Linux together with a brief manual.
steps_raw:
BurstDECONV is a novel statistical inference method that deconvolves signal traces into individual  transcription initiation events.  We use the distribution of waiting times between successive polymerase initiation  events to identify mechanistic features of transcription   
such as the number of rate-limiting steps and their kinetics. This repository contains the BurstDECONV graphical user interface implemented for Windows and Linux together with a brief manual.
keywords: fluorescence imaging, live transcription imaging, transcriptional bursting, machine learning
